organization.name: LIFTS
organization.full_name: Launch Initiatives for Technologies in Space
organization.institution: University of Puerto Rico at Mayaguez
organization.tagline: Advancing near-space research and inspiring the next generation of aerospace engineers from Puerto Rico.
organization.description: LIFTS is a student-led near-space research program at UPRM conducting high-altitude balloon missions, payload development, and CubeSat research.

nexo.name: NEXO
nexo.full_name: Space Navigator for Exploration and Observation
nexo.recovery_info: Recovered intact near Tyler, TX, about 200 miles from launch
nexo.summary: Our first high-altitude balloon launch during the 2024 total solar eclipse as part of the Nationwide Eclipse Ballooning Project.
highlights: Completed eclipse-day launch operations in Austin, Texas.
Tracked the payload through a multi-hour near-space flight.
Recovered the flight system intact near Tyler, Texas.
objectives: Collect near-space imagery and environmental data during eclipse conditions.
Validate team operations for launch, tracking, and recovery.
Build the foundation for future Puerto Rico-led balloon missions.

ascent.name: ASCENT
ascent.full_name: Advanced Science Carrier for Environmental and Near-space Technology
ascent.summary: A Puerto Rico-based high-altitude balloon mission carrying multiple scientific payloads into the stratosphere.
highlights: Supports five payload modules in a 2.5 kg payload envelope.
Expands LIFTS launch operations from mainland campaign work to Puerto Rico.
objectives: Collect environmental data through ascent, float, and descent.
Validate payload integration and recovery systems.
Train the next mission operations cohort at UPRM.

cubesat.name: CubeSat Program
cubesat.summary: A long-range development program to design and build a student-led CubeSat at UPRM.
highlights: Building subsystem experience through balloon payload work.
Targeting a future low Earth orbit mission.
objectives: Develop flight software, power, communications, structures, and payload subsystems.
Create a repeatable student satellite engineering pipeline.
Prepare the university team for future launch opportunities.

programs.name: High-Altitude Balloon Program
programs.description: Near-space flights that carry student-built payloads to the stratosphere.

programs.name: CubeSat Development
programs.description: Subsystem research and integration toward a future UPRM student satellite.

programs.name: Mission Operations
programs.description: Launch planning, tracking, recovery, logistics, and field operations training.

updates.title: ASCENT mission planning continues
updates.summary: The team is refining payload interfaces, launch operations, and recovery procedures for ASCENT.

updates.title: CubeSat program enters subsystem study phase
updates.summary: Students are mapping requirements for power, communications, flight software, and structures.

updates.title: NEXO recovery validates operations workflow
updates.summary: The NEXO mission gave LIFTS hands-on launch, tracking, recovery, and post-flight analysis experience.

careers.title: Payload Engineer
careers.description: Design, assemble, and test scientific payloads for balloon and satellite missions.

careers.title: Flight Software Developer
careers.description: Build embedded and ground-support software for data collection, telemetry, and mission operations.

careers.title: Operations Coordinator
careers.description: Support launch planning, safety checklists, logistics, communications, and recovery procedures.

careers.title: Communications Designer
careers.description: Help translate mission work into visuals, articles, social posts, and sponsor-ready material.

contributors.name: University of Puerto Rico at Mayaguez

faqs.question: Who can join LIFTS?
faqs.answer: Students interested in aerospace, electronics, software, operations, outreach, and research are welcome to connect with the team.

faqs.question: Do I need prior aerospace experience?
faqs.answer: No. LIFTS is built around learning by doing, mentorship, and hands-on mission work.

faqs.question: How can sponsors or collaborators reach the team?
faqs.answer: Use the contact page or email the team directly to discuss mission support, partnerships, media, or technical collaboration.

cards.title: General
cards.description: Questions, student interest, and mission information.
